message:
fix(circuit_breaker): clamp half_open_max_probes to 24-bit maximum

The circuit breaker state machine packs the probe counter into 24 bits,
meaning any value above 16,777,215 (0x00FF_FFFF) silently wraps to zero
during to_bits()/from_bits() round-trips. This caused the breaker to
incorrectly permit probes beyond the configured limit when the counter
overflowed.

Changes:
- Add MAX_HALF_OPEN_PROBES constant (0x00FF_FFFF = 16,777,215)
- Clamp the builder's half_open_max_probes() setter to this maximum,
  using a const-compatible if/else rather than u32::min() to preserve
  const fn eligibility
- Add builder_clamps_max_probes test verifying that values > 2^24 are
  silently clamped to the representable maximum
- Document the clamping behavior on both the policy field and the
  builder method

This is the fix for the overflow documented in the companion repro test
(tests/circuit_breaker_repro.rs).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
